title: kielmeyera rubriflora var. typica
intro: Kielmeyera rubriflora var. typica, also known as the 'Coral Tree', is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Calophyllaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating gastrointestinal problems. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Kielmeyera rubriflora var. typica are typically used to make infusions, teas, or decoctions, which are then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
